title: adenium obesum subsp. multiflorum
intro: Adenium obesum subsp. multiflorum, also known as the Desert Rose or Sabji, is a succulent plant species belonging to the Apocynaceae family, characterized by its thick, water-storing stems and vibrant flowers. Traditionally, it has been used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its medicinal properties attributed to the presence of flavonoids, alkaloids, and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Adenium obesum subsp. multiflorum include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. In herbal preparations, the plant's extracts or dried flowers are often used in traditional remedies, such as teas, infusions, and topical creams, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
